steps.title: Match the growth factor
steps.content: For $f(x)=k(1.72)^x$, increasing $x$ by $1$ multiplies the output by $1.72$. The new function must multiply by $1.72$ when $x$ increases by $\frac{1}{3}$.

steps.title: Set the new base
steps.content:
If $g(x)=ka^x$, then increasing $x$ by $\frac{1}{3}$ multiplies the output by $a^{1/3}$. So
$$
a^{1/3}=1.72
$$
$$
a=(1.72)^3
$$

steps.title: Rewrite in choice form
steps.content: Use exponent rules to rewrite the function in the same form as the choices. $$ g(x)=k((1.72)^3)^x=k(1.72)^{3x}=k(1.72^x)^3 $$ The answer is B.
